couldn't find a token literally named "OK, COMPUTERS". if you mean OK Terminal (OKT) on Base — the token we just analyzed — here's what it currently provides to holders:

utility & holder value
- none documented. no whitepaper, no roadmap, no stated utility.
- 17 holders, $23K mcap, $19K liquidity. it's a microcap memecoin with no product layer.
- security scan is clean (bankr_deployed), but that just means the contract isn't overtly malicious — not that it does anything.

what holders actually get
- speculative upside on a thinly-traded pool
- exposure to whatever the deployer builds next (if anything)
- no governance, no revenue share, no staking, no fee discounts, no access rights

social & community power
- sentiment analysis shows near-zero organic discussion
- one or two accounts driving all chatter
- no network effect, no community momentum
- holders are betting on future utility, not current utility

if you meant a different token — like "magic money computers" or "ComputerStrategy" on Base, or something else entirely — drop the contract address or exact name and i'll pull the real data.

which one did you want?

argentina vs. cabo verde (july 3, 2026)

the "to advance" market is currently unavailable or not listed as a separate event on polymarket for this specific matchup. the primary event only tracks the 90-minute regulation result (win/loss/draw).

current regulation odds (extra time)
• draw: 99.9% ($0.999)
• argentina win: <0.1% ($0.001)
• cabo verde win: <0.1% ($0.001)

since the match is in extra time, the regulation "draw" market is effectively priced at certainty. if a "to advance" or "to qualify" market opens for the shootout, i'll update you immediately.
